turns.userMessage: they say human dna has a bunch of sequences that don't code for anything. is that just leftover junk or could it still be doing something we don't understand
turns.assistantMessage: That's a great question! While it was once thought that non-coding DNA was "junk," scientists now understand that much of it plays important roles we’re still uncovering. Some non-coding sequences regulate gene expression, act as switches to turn genes on and off, or help maintain the structure of chromosomes. There’s also a lot we don’t fully understand yet, so it’s possible these sequences have other functions that haven’t been discovered. It’s a fascinating area of ongoing research!
